question_text: Two boxes are stacked on top of each other on Earth. Box A (on top) has a mass of 5 kg, and Box B (on bottom) has a mass of 10 kg. Using g = 10 N/kg, calculate the total weight of both boxes and determine the normal force that the ground must exert on Box B to keep the system in equilibrium.
correct_answer: Total weight = 150 N. Normal force from ground on Box B = 150 N.
explanation: Total mass = 5 + 10 = 15 kg, so total weight = 15 × 10 = 150 N. For the system to be in equilibrium (not accelerating), the upward normal force from the ground must equal the total downward weight force, which is 150 N.
